## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On September 22, 1862, US President Abraham Lincoln achieved an incredibly massive, historically profound, and absolute defining milestone of the American Civil War. He officially and aggressively issued the preliminary Emancipation Proclamation. This massive, highly strategic executive order aggressively declared that all slaves in Confederate-held territory would be absolutely and forever free on January 1, 1863. This incredibly massive moral and political decree fundamentally and aggressively transformed the massive war into an absolute crusade to completely destroy the institution of slavery.

On September 22, 1980, the incredibly massive, fiercely defiant, and historically crucial Solidarity movement was officially and aggressively founded in Poland. Heavily led by the incredibly charismatic Lech Wałęsa, it aggressively became the absolute, very first independent trade union legally recognized within the massive, deeply oppressive Warsaw Pact. This massive, highly organized grassroots resistance fiercely championed absolute workers' rights, fundamentally sparking the massive, deeply powerful anti-communist revolution that would aggressively destroy Soviet hegemony in Eastern Europe a decade later.
